office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Sam Low faces nurse and union organizer Kathryn Lewandowsky and challenger Dusty Wisniew, whose reviewed platform evidence is sparse.

candidates.summary: Wisniew is a challenger whose official records establish his candidacy but no detailed legislative platform.
Lewandowsky is a registered nurse and union organizer emphasizing health care, labor rights, reproductive rights, gun-violence prevention, and rural investment.
highlights: Supports health-care access, labor rights, and rural investment [S2]
Supports reproductive rights and gun-violence prevention [S2]

spending.basis: She explicitly supports investment in rural communities.

safety.basis: She emphasizes gun-violence prevention rather than enforcement.

experience.basis: She has elected union-unit and grievance-officer experience but no public-office record.

healthcare.basis: She supports health-care access without proposing a universal public or single-payer system.

candidates.summary: Low is an incumbent representative and former county and city councilmember emphasizing tax restraint, rural housing supply, infrastructure, and small business.
highlights: Supports tax restraint and rural housing supply [S2]
Brings state, county, and city elected experience [S1][S2]

housing.basis: He explicitly emphasizes rural housing supply.

experience.basis: He is an incumbent representative and former county and city councilmember.
